בְּרֵאשִׁית: The house (Beth) holds the chief (Resh) ox-strength (Aleph), consumed by fire (Shin), worked by hand (Yod), sealed with a covenant mark (Tav) — the inside of the first moment, before anything existed.
בָּרָא: The house (Beth) and the chief (Resh) ox (Aleph) — strength brought out from inside, the act of a master builder pulling something from within himself into existence.
אֱלֹהִים: The ox (Aleph) goaded forward (Lamed), revealed by breath (He), working through hands (Yod) over the waters (Mem) — the mighty one who drives, reveals, and rules the chaos.
אֵת: Ox-strength (Aleph) sealed by the covenant mark (Tav) — the strong one bound by the sign, nearness as the power that draws close under promise.
הַשָּׁמַיִם: Behold (He) the teeth/fire (Shin) consuming the waters (Mem), worked by hands (Yod) into waters again (Mem) — the heavens as fire and water stretched above, the burning expanse.
הָאָרֶץ: Behold (He) the ox-strength (Aleph) at the head (Resh) sealed by covenant (Tav) — the strong foundation marked and bounded, the earth as a covenanted place.
תֹהוּ: The covenant mark (Tav) breathes out (He) and connects to nothing (Vav) — a signed emptiness, a bound void, formlessness that is somehow real.
וָבֹהוּ: Connected (Vav) to the house (Beth) that breathes (He) and connects again (Vav) — an echoing emptiness inside a hollow dwelling, void answering void.
וְחֹשֶׁךְ: The fence/inner room (Chet) seen by the eye (Ayin) and bent by an open palm (Kaph) — darkness as a closed inner space that presses and covers.
פְּנֵי: The mouth (Pe) of the living fish (Nun), worked by a hand (Yod) — the speaking, breathing surface turned outward, the face as the active presence.
תְהוֹם: The covenant mark (Tav) breathes (He) over the eye (Ayin) of the waters (Mem) — the abyss as a covenanted deep that sees and swallows.
וְרוּחַ: The head/chief (Resh) hooked (Vav) through the fence (Chet) — the breath that crosses the boundary, the spirit hooking through the barrier between worlds.
מְרַחֶפֶת: The waters (Mem) at the head (Resh) inside the fence (Chet), speaking (Pe) sealed by covenant (Tav) — the mighty one hovering at the threshold, trembling over the boundary.
הַמָּיִם: Behold (He) the waters (Mem), worked by hand (Yod), and waters again (Mem) — the revealed double-waters, chaos spread above and below.
וַיֹּאמֶר: Hooked (Vav) by the working hand (Yod), the ox-strength (Aleph) rises from the waters (Mem) as a chief (Resh) — the divine word surging up and going forth.
יְהִי: Hand/work (Yod) breathes (He) through another hand (Yod) — divine action breathing itself into being, work calling itself into existence.
אוֹר: The ox-strength (Aleph) connected (Vav) to the head (Resh) — the first power hooked to the highest point, strength flowing from its source, illumination.
וַיְהִי: Connected (Vav), the hand works (Yod), the breath reveals (He), the hand works again (Yod) — and the deed was done, the action was completed.
וַיַּרְא: Connected (Vav), the hand (Yod) of the chief (Resh) ox-strength (Aleph) — the powerful hand of the leader turned to look, divine sight reaching out.
הָאוֹר: Behold (He), the ox-strength (Aleph) hooked (Vav) to the chief (Resh) — see it: the primal power connected to its source, light made visible.
כִּי: The open palm (Kaph) working (Yod) — the hand that yields and acts, the gesture of acknowledgment that makes something true.
טוֹב: The coiled snake (Tet) hooked (Vav) inside the house (Beth) — goodness as the contained potential safely connected within the dwelling, the gathered blessing kept inside the home.
וַיַּבְדֵּל: Connected (Vav), the hand (Yod) of the house (Beth) enters the door (Dalet) guided by the goad (Lamed) — the separating hand moving through the doorway to divide.
בֵּין: The house (Beth) with the working hand (Yod) of the living fish (Nun) — the active living space between two things, the dwelling place that separates one from another.
וַיִּקְרָא: Connected (Vav), the hand (Yod) follows the sun (Qoph) past the head (Resh) back to the ox (Aleph) — calling out, the voice following all the way back to the source.
יוֹם: The hand/work (Yod) hooked (Vav) to the waters (Mem) — the working deed connected over the mighty waters, the span of light over the deep.
לַיְלָה: The goad (Lamed) works (Yod) and the goad returns (Lamed) breathing (He) — the directed motion that goes out and comes back, the cycle that turns.
עֶרֶב: The eye (Ayin) sees the head (Resh) entering the house (Beth) — the watching of the chief going into the dwelling as light fades, dusk.
בֹקֶר: The house (Beth) follows the sun (Qoph) to the chief (Resh) — the dwelling chasing the horizon toward the head, the moment light breaks from the house of night.
אֶחָד: The ox-strength (Aleph) enclosed by the fence (Chet) entering the door (Dalet) — primal strength contained and then moving through, the one that stands alone and advances.
רָקִיעַ: The head/chief (Resh) follows the sun on the horizon (Qoph), worked by the hand (Yod), seen by the eye (Ayin) — the expanse stretched to the horizon and perceived.
בְּתוֹךְ: The house (Beth) marked by covenant (Tav), hooked (Vav) and bent by the open palm (Kaph) — what is sealed inside and held in the yielding hand, the midst.
שָׁמַיִם: The teeth/fire (Shin) consuming the waters (Mem), worked by hand (Yod) into waters (Mem) — fire pressed into water pressed into fire, the burning expanse of heaven.
שֵׁנִי: The teeth (Shin) of the living fish (Nun) worked by the hand (Yod) — the consuming life that works again, the second that repeats and follows.
מָקוֹם: The waters (Mem) follow the sun (Qoph), hooked (Vav) back to the waters (Mem) — the gathered waters finding their horizon and returning, a bounded place where things stand.
הַיַּבָּשָׁה: Behold (He), the working hand (Yod) inside the house (Beth) consumed by teeth (Shin) and breathing (He) — what the fire leaves behind inside the dwelling, the dried and hardened ground.
אֶרֶץ: The ox-strength (Aleph) at the head (Resh) caught by the fish-hook (Tsade) — the strong foundation led by the chief and anchored in desire, the land that holds fast.
יַמִּים: The hand (Yod) of the waters (Mem) and waters (Mem) again, worked by the hand (Yod) into more waters (Mem) — hands multiplied in the many waters, the roiling seas.
דֶּשֶׁא: The door (Dalet) opened by the fire/teeth (Shin) back to the ox-strength (Aleph) — the doorway that fire opens to first strength, new life pushing up from the ground.
עֵשֶׂב: The eye (Ayin) of the consuming teeth (Shin) inside the house (Beth) — what the devouring eye sees growing inside the dwelling, the herb that feeds.
זֶרַע: The weapon (Zayin) of the chief (Resh) seen by the eye (Ayin) — the seed is a buried blade placed by the head into sight, the cutting potential waiting to be perceived.
עֵץ: The eye (Ayin) of the fish hook (Tsade) — what is seen rooted deep and reaching upward with desire, the tree as the standing witness that hooks into the earth.
פְּרִי: The mouth (Pe) of the chief (Resh) worked by the hand (Yod) — what the leader's speech produces through labor, the fruit of effort and word.
מְאֹרֹת: The waters (Mem) of the ox-strength (Aleph) at the chief (Resh) sealed by covenant (Tav) — mighty lights covenanted to rule from the highest point over the expanse.
לְאֹתֹת: The goad (Lamed) drives the ox (Aleph) to the covenant mark (Tav) and mark again (Tav) — directed toward the sign and sealed again — appointed signs.
מוֹעֲדִים: The waters (Mem) hooked (Vav) to the eye (Ayin) entering the door (Dalet), worked by the hand (Yod) in the waters (Mem) — the mighty eye at the threshold watching the seasons turn.
שָׁנִים: The teeth (Shin) of the living fish (Nun) worked by the hand (Yod) in the waters (Mem) — the consuming cycle of life in the waters, one year devouring the next.
הַגָּדֹל: Behold (He) the camel (Gimel) entering the door (Dalet) guided by the goad (Lamed) — the great lifting and carrying that pushes through the threshold, greatness in motion.
הַכּוֹכָבִים: Behold (He) the open palm (Kaph) hooked (Vav) to the palm (Kaph) inside the house (Beth), worked by the hand (Yod) in the waters (Mem) — the yielded hands inside the heavenly house, the stars as bending lights.
נֶפֶשׁ: The living fish (Nun) breathes through the mouth (Pe) consumed by the teeth (Shin) — the swimming life that speaks and is devoured, the soul as the animated breathing creature.
חַיָּה: The fence/inner room (Chet) worked by the hand (Yod) and revealed by breath (He) — life as what the hand produces inside the bounded space, the living thing.
הָעוֹף: Behold (He) the eye (Ayin) hooked (Vav) to the mouth (Pe) — the seeing mouth that opens upward, the bird as the creature of sight and cry.
נַעֲשֶׂה: The living fish (Nun) is seen by the eye (Ayin) consumed by fire (Shin) and revealed by breath (He) — let the living be seen, consumed, and brought forth.
אָדָם: The ox-strength (Aleph) entering the door (Dalet) into the waters (Mem) — the strong one stepping through the threshold into the chaos, man as the ruddy one formed from the earth.
בְּצַלְמֵנוּ: The house (Beth) of the fish-hook desire (Tsade), guided by the goad (Lamed), in the mighty waters (Mem) of the living fish (Nun) hooked (Vav) — the image as the hunted likeness inside the dwelling.
זָכָר: The weapon (Zayin) bent by the open palm (Kaph) at the chief (Resh) — the cutting distinction yielded at the head, what is marked and remembered at the top, the male as the one marked and recalled.
וּנְקֵבָה: Connected (Vav), the living fish (Nun) follows the sun (Qoph) inside the house (Beth) breathing (He) — the life that follows the horizon into the dwelling, the pierced vessel.
וַיְבָרֶךְ: Connected (Vav), the hand (Yod) of the house (Beth) at the chief (Resh) yields the palm (Kaph) — the kneeling of the house before the chief, blessing as bending the knee.
פְּרוּ: The mouth (Pe) of the chief (Resh) hooked (Vav) — the word of the leader connected and going forth, fruit as the speech of the head made real.
וּרְבוּ: Connected (Vav), the chief (Resh) inside the house (Beth) hooks (Vav) again — the head multiplying inside the dwelling and connecting outward, increase filling the house.
הַמָּאוֹר: Behold (He) the waters (Mem) of ox-strength (Aleph) hooked (Vav) to the chief (Resh) — the mighty primal light connected to its source, the luminary as channeled power.
שְׁלִישִׁי: The teeth (Shin) goad (Lamed) the hand (Yod), then the teeth (Shin) work the hand (Yod) again — consumed, directed, worked, consumed again — the third as the doubled process.
מְאֹד: The waters (Mem) of ox-strength (Aleph) entering the door (Dalet) — the mighty force crossing the threshold, power flooding through the opening, exceedingly, to the uttermost.
הַשִּׁשִּׁי: Behold (He) the teeth (Shin) consuming the teeth (Shin) worked by the hand (Yod) — fire consuming fire under the working hand, the sixth as doubled pressure.
וַיַּעַשׂ: Connected (Vav), the hand (Yod) of the eye (Ayin) consumed by fire (Shin) — the seeing hand pressed through by fire, the making that requires both vision and burning effort.
עַל: The eye (Ayin) of the goad (Lamed) — the seeing that directs from above, perception aimed downward from the high place.
כָּל: The open palm (Kaph) of the goad (Lamed) — the yielded hand directed outward toward everything, the encompassing reach.
אֲשֶׁר: The ox-strength (Aleph) consumed by fire (Shin) at the head (Resh) — the primal power pressed through the flame to the chief, who as the one identified and singled out.
וַתּוֹצֵא: Connected (Vav), the covenant mark (Tav) hooked (Vav) to the prop/support (Samech) of the ox-strength (Aleph) — the sealed support of primal strength bringing something out and upholding it.
יְהֹוָה: The working hand (Yod) reveals through the window (He), hooks and connects (Vav), and reveals again (He) — the self-existent one whose deeds are seen, joined, and beheld without end.
אֵל: The ox-strength (Aleph) driven by the goad (Lamed) — the mighty one directed with purpose, God as the primal power that moves all things forward.
כֹּל: The open palm (Kaph) of the goad (Lamed) — the yielded hand directed outward toward everything, the encompassing reach.
אָמַר: The ox-strength (Aleph) surges through the waters (Mem) to the chief (Resh) — power rising from the depths to the head, the authoritative word spoken from the source.
לֹא: The goad (Lamed) turned back from the ox (Aleph) — direction that refuses the strong one, the shepherd's staff that blocks and forbids.
בֵּן: The house (Beth) of the living fish (Nun) — life continuing inside the family dwelling, the one who carries the household forward.
לוֹ: The goad (Lamed) hooked (Vav) — direction fastened and secured, the staff that connects and points toward him, belonging.
הָיָה: Behold through the window (He), the hand works (Yod), behold again (He) — breath and deed bracketed by revelation, the act of becoming visible, of coming into existence.
עָשָׂה: The eye (Ayin) perceives, the teeth (Shin) consume, and the breath (He) reveals — to see what must be done, devour the raw material, and bring forth the finished work.
בּוֹא: The house (Beth) hooked (Vav) to the ox-strength (Aleph) — the dwelling connected to the strong one arriving, the entering of power into the home.
מֶלֶךְ: The mighty waters (Mem) goaded (Lamed) by the open palm (Kaph) — a king is the one who directs the overwhelming flood with a steady hand, the ruler who guides chaos with authority.
יִשְׂרָאֵל: The hand (Yod) consumes by fire (Shin) at the head (Resh) of the ox-strength (Aleph) goaded (Lamed) — the working hand that wrestles, prevails at the top, and directs the mighty one.
אִישׁ: The ox-strength (Aleph) working by hand (Yod) and consuming with teeth (Shin) — the strong one who labors and devours, the man defined by his strength and toil.
פָּנִים: The mouth (Pe) of the living fish (Nun), worked by the hand (Yod) over the waters (Mem) — the speaking, breathing surface turned outward, the face as the active presence.
בַּיִת: The house (Beth) worked by the hand (Yod) and sealed with a covenant mark (Tav) — a dwelling built by labor and marked by promise, the home as a covenanted place.
נָתַן: The living fish (Nun) sealed by the covenant mark (Tav) and the living fish (Nun) again — life marked and given, then life received again, the cycle of giving that multiplies.
הוּא: Behold (He) the hook (Vav) of the ox-strength (Aleph) — see the connection to the strong one, the one who is pointed to and recognized.
עַם: The eye (Ayin) over the waters (Mem) — the multitude perceived as a mighty sea, people as a vast flood seen from a height.
יָד: The hand (Yod) at the door (Dalet) — the working deed that opens the pathway, the hand as the instrument that enters and grasps.
דָּבָר: The door (Dalet) of the house (Beth) opened by the chief (Resh) — what comes through the threshold from within, the word as something released from the inner chamber of the head.
בִי: The house (Beth) of the working hand (Yod) — what is inside the deed, the dwelling of action, the plea that comes from within.
רָאָה: The head (Resh) of ox-strength (Aleph) revealed by breath (He) — the chief turning with power and beholding through the window, sight as strength made manifest.
עַד: The eye (Ayin) at the door (Dalet) — perception reaching to the threshold, the gaze that stretches as far as the boundary.
מִן: The waters (Mem) of the living fish (Nun) — the mighty source from which life swims, the origin and separation, from.
אָב: The ox-strength (Aleph) of the house (Beth) — the strong one inside the dwelling, the leader who shelters and protects the family.
זֶה: The weapon (Zayin) revealed by breath (He) — the cutting edge made visible, this thing right here, pointed to and laid bare.
שָׁמַע: The teeth (Shin) over the waters (Mem) perceiving through the eye (Ayin) — the consuming fire washing over the deep and seeing, hearing as the devouring attention that perceives everything.
דָבַר: The door (Dalet) of the house (Beth) at the head (Resh) — the pathway through the inner dwelling to the chief, to speak as arranging words from the chamber of thought.
עִיר: The eye (Ayin) of the working hand (Yod) at the head (Resh) — a watched place where hands labor under the chief, the city as the guarded seat of authority.
יָשַׁב: The hand (Yod) consumed by fire (Shin) inside the house (Beth) — the working one who endures the pressing heat within the dwelling, to sit and remain in one's home.
דָּוִד: The door (Dalet) hooked (Vav) to the door (Dalet) — two thresholds joined, the beloved who bridges one entrance to another, the connector of pathways.
יָצָא: The hand (Yod) hooked by the fish-hook (Tsade) to the ox-strength (Aleph) — the working deed pulled forth by desire toward the strong one, the going out that draws power forward.
שׁוּב: The teeth (Shin) hooked (Vav) to the house (Beth) — the consuming one fastened back to the dwelling, the fire that turns and returns to its home.
יָלַךְ: The hand (Yod) goaded (Lamed) by the open palm (Kaph) — the working deed driven forward by the yielding hand, walking as directed motion toward a destination.
עִם: The eye (Ayin) in the waters (Mem) — to see alongside the mighty current, presence as standing together in the same flood.
לָקַח: The goad (Lamed) follows the sun on the horizon (Qoph) into the fenced enclosure (Chet) — the directed pursuit of what is seen at the horizon and brought into the inner room, the act of taking.
יָדַע: The hand (Yod) at the door (Dalet) perceiving through the eye (Ayin) — the working deed that opens the threshold to sight, knowing as touching the doorway and seeing what lies beyond.
אִם: The ox-strength (Aleph) of the waters (Mem) — the mighty force of the deep that may or may not surge, the conditional power, if.
עָלָה: The eye (Ayin) goaded upward (Lamed) and revealed by breath (He) — perception driven higher and made visible, the ascending that rises into view.
שָׁנֶה: The teeth (Shin) of the living fish (Nun) revealed by breath (He) — the consuming cycle of life made visible, the year as the repetition that devours and reveals.
אֲנִי: The ox-strength (Aleph) of the living fish (Nun) worked by the hand (Yod) — the primal self swimming forward and laboring, the I that is strong, alive, and active.
עַיִן: The eye (Ayin) worked by the hand (Yod) of the living fish (Nun) — the perceiving organ shaped by labor and life, the eye as the fountain from which seeing flows.
שֵׁם: The teeth (Shin) over the waters (Mem) — the consuming fire that marks the surface of the deep, a name as the indelible brand pressed onto the chaos of identity.
שָׁלַח: The teeth (Shin) goaded (Lamed) through the fence (Chet) — the consuming force driven past the barrier, to send something beyond the enclosure and out into the open.
הִנֵּה: Behold (He), the living fish (Nun) revealed again (He) — the window opens to life and the window opens again, the double revelation that demands attention.
מוּת: The waters (Mem) hooked (Vav) to the covenant mark (Tav) — the mighty chaos fastened to the final sign, death as the flood that reaches the last boundary.
שָׁם: The teeth (Shin) in the waters (Mem) — the consuming fire submerged in the deep, a distant place where things are pressed below the surface, there.
יְהוּדָה: The hand (Yod) reveals (He), hooks (Vav) through the door (Dalet), and reveals again (He) — the working praise that opens the window, passes the threshold, and is beheld, Judah as the praised one.
אָכַל: The ox-strength (Aleph) bent by the open palm (Kaph) and goaded (Lamed) — the strong one yielding to the hand that directs toward the mouth, eating as power brought under the guiding staff.
עֶבֶד: The eye (Ayin) inside the house (Beth) at the door (Dalet) — the one who watches within the dwelling and stands at the threshold, the servant who sees and waits at the entry.
אַיִן: The ox-strength (Aleph) worked by the hand (Yod) of the living fish (Nun) — the strong one whose labor and life have dissolved, nothing as the ox that no longer moves.
אִשָּׁה: The ox-strength (Aleph) consumed by fire (Shin) and revealed by breath (He) — the strong one who burns and is made visible, woman as the fire that illuminates the dwelling.
גַּם: The camel (Gimel) over the waters (Mem) — the carrier that lifts above the mighty flood, also as the one who gathers even this into the load.
שְׁנַיִם: The teeth (Shin) of the living fish (Nun) worked by the hand (Yod) in the waters (Mem) — the consuming cycle of life doubled in labor over the deep, two as the pair that repeats.
מֹשֶׁה: The waters (Mem) consumed by teeth (Shin) and revealed by breath (He) — the one drawn from the chaos and made visible, Moses as the life pulled from the devouring flood.
כֹּהֵן: The open palm (Kaph) revealed by breath (He) with the life of the fish (Nun) — the yielded hand made visible through living service, the priest as the one whose bended knee brings life.
מָה: The waters (Mem) revealed by breath (He) — the chaos made visible through the window, the question that opens the flood of unknowing.
אֵלֶּה: The ox-strength (Aleph) goaded (Lamed) and revealed by breath (He) — the strong one directed and made visible, these things pointed out and brought into the light.
אַתָּה: The ox-strength (Aleph) sealed by the covenant mark (Tav) and revealed by breath (He) — the strong one marked and beheld, thou as the one who stands identified and addressed face to face.
כֵּן: The open palm (Kaph) of the living fish (Nun) — the yielded hand set upright with life, the steady base that holds firm and says so, thus, established.
קָרָא: The sun on the horizon (Qoph) at the head (Resh) of the ox-strength (Aleph) — the dawn following the chief back to the source, to call out as the voice that reaches from the horizon to the first.
אַל: The ox-strength (Aleph) stopped by the goad (Lamed) — the strong one halted by the directing staff, the prohibition that turns power aside.
אַחַר: The ox-strength (Aleph) behind the fence (Chet) of the chief (Resh) — the strong one enclosed behind the barrier of the head, what follows after and trails behind.
דֶּרֶךְ: The door (Dalet) of the chief (Resh) bent by the open palm (Kaph) — the pathway that the head follows and the hand shapes, the road as a trodden way pressed open by the leader.
נָשָׂא: The living fish (Nun) consumed by fire (Shin) toward the ox-strength (Aleph) — the swimming life pressed through the flames toward primal power, to lift as bearing a burning burden upward.
יְרוּשָׁלִַ͏ם: The hand (Yod) of the chief (Resh) hooked (Vav) to the consuming fire (Shin), goaded (Lamed) into the waters (Mem) — the founded city where the head's work connects through fire and is directed over the mighty deep, Jerusalem as the teaching of peace.
מִצְרַיִם: The waters (Mem) caught by the fish-hook (Tsade) at the head (Resh), worked by the hand (Yod) in waters (Mem) again — the mighty flood that hooks and confines the chief between two seas, Egypt as the narrow place of double pressure.
אָח: The ox-strength (Aleph) inside the fence (Chet) — the strong one enclosed within the same wall, a brother as the power that shares the inner room.
קוּם: The sun on the horizon (Qoph) hooked (Vav) to the waters (Mem) — the dawn fastened to the deep, rising as the light that pulls itself up from the mighty flood.
זֹאת: The weapon (Zayin) of the ox-strength (Aleph) sealed by covenant (Tav) — the cutting edge of the first one marked and identified, this as the thing pointed to and claimed.
רֹאשׁ: The head (Resh) of ox-strength (Aleph) consuming with teeth (Shin) — the chief of the strong ones who devours, the head as the summit that takes in everything beneath it.
לֵב: The goad (Lamed) inside the house (Beth) — the directing staff within the dwelling, the heart as the inner authority that drives from the deepest room.
בַּת: The house (Beth) marked by covenant (Tav) — the dwelling sealed with the sign of the family, the daughter as the branch of the home that carries the mark forward.
שׂוּם: The teeth (Shin) hooked (Vav) to the waters (Mem) — the consuming edge fastened into the deep, to put something in place by pressing it down into the surface.
מַיִם: The waters (Mem) worked by the hand (Yod) and waters (Mem) again — the mighty deep labored over and returned to, water as the doubled chaos that surrounds and flows.
מֵאָה: The waters (Mem) of ox-strength (Aleph) revealed by breath (He) — the mighty power made visible and multiplied, a hundred as the full measure of strength beheld.
כֹּה: The open palm (Kaph) revealed by breath (He) — the yielded hand made visible through the window, like this as the gesture that shows and points.
הֵם: Behold (He) the waters (Mem) — the revealed multitude, they as the mighty flood made visible, the many seen through the window.
גּוֹי: The camel (Gimel) hooked (Vav) to the working hand (Yod) — the carrier connected to labor, a nation as the body that lifts, joins, and works together.
עָבַר: The eye (Ayin) inside the house (Beth) toward the chief (Resh) — the perceiving one passing through the dwelling toward the head, to cross over as moving through the inner space to the other side.
הַר: Behold (He) the chief (Resh) — the breath that sees the head rising above, the mountain as the visible summit that stands over the land.
גָּדוֹל: The camel (Gimel) at the door (Dalet) hooked (Vav) to the goad (Lamed) — the great carrier at the threshold connected to the directing staff, greatness as the lifting force driven forward.
עָמַד: The eye (Ayin) over the waters (Mem) at the door (Dalet) — the perceiving one planted above the flood at the threshold, standing as the watchful presence that holds its ground.
הָלַךְ: Behold (He), the goad (Lamed) yields to the palm (Kaph) — the revealed direction bending the open hand forward, walking as the visible journey guided step by step.
תַּחַת: The covenant mark (Tav) fenced in (Chet) by the covenant mark (Tav) — the sign enclosed within the barrier and sealed again, beneath as the place bounded above and below by promise.
קוֹל: The sun on the horizon (Qoph) hooked (Vav) to the goad (Lamed) — the dawn connected to the directing staff, the voice as the cry that follows from the edge of the world and drives forward.
אֶלֶף: The ox-strength (Aleph) goaded (Lamed) through the mouth (Pe) — the strong one driven forward and declared aloud, a thousand as the fullness of might spoken into being.
נָכָה: The living fish (Nun) bent by the open palm (Kaph) and revealed by breath (He) — the swimming life struck down by the yielding hand and made visible, the blow that fells and exposes.
פֶּה: The mouth (Pe) breathes through the window (He) — the opening that speaks and exhales, the mouth as a gateway where words and breath pour out into the world.
יָלַד: The hand (Yod) goads forward (Lamed) through the door (Dalet) — the working hand that drives new life through the threshold, the labor that pushes a child into the open.
צָוָה: The fish hook (Tsade) fastens (Vav) and is revealed (He) — desire cast out, pinned in place, and made visible to all, the command as a binding order hung in the window for everyone to see.
עוֹד: The eye (Ayin) hooks (Vav) back to the door (Dalet) — the gaze that keeps connecting to the same threshold, returning again and again, the persistence of one more time.
שָׁמַר: The consuming teeth (Shin) guard the waters (Mem) at the head (Resh) — fire standing watch over the mighty deep, the sharp vigilance of the one who keeps and preserves.
קֹדֶשׁ: The sun on the horizon (Qoph) enters the door (Dalet) consumed by fire (Shin) — what follows the light through the sacred threshold and is purified by flame, the holy set apart by burning.
מָצָא: The mighty waters (Mem) cast the fish hook (Tsade) toward the ox-strength (Aleph) — desire searching through the chaos until it hooks the strong one, the finding of what was sought in the deep.
אֲדֹנָי: The ox-strength (Aleph) enters the door (Dalet), giving life (Nun) by the working hand (Yod) — the strong one who crosses every threshold, teeming with life and active in deed, the sovereign Lord.
עוֹלָם: The eye (Ayin) hooks (Vav) and goads (Lamed) into the waters (Mem) — the gaze that connects to the directing rod plunging into the boundless deep, eternity as what the eye cannot find the end of.
נָפַל: The living fish (Nun) speaks at the edge (Pe) and is goaded down (Lamed) — the swimming life that cries out as the rod drives it downward, the fall from living motion to the ground.
עַתָּה: The eye (Ayin) marks the covenant (Tav) and beholds it (He) — the moment of seeing the sign right now, the present instant when perception meets the mark.
רַב: The head/chief (Resh) fills the house (Beth) — the great one whose presence overflows the dwelling, abundance as the chief who makes the household teem.
שָׁלוֹשׁ: The consuming teeth (Shin) goad (Lamed) and hook (Vav) to consuming teeth again (Shin) — fire directed and connected to more fire, the third strand that binds two together.
מִי: The waters (Mem) worked by the hand (Yod) — a hand reaching into the chaos and asking who is there, the question that searches the mighty unknown.
מִשְׁפָּט: The waters (Mem) are consumed by teeth (Shin), spoken by the mouth (Pe), and coiled by the serpent (Tet) — chaos devoured, declared aloud, and bound in its place, the verdict that contains disorder.
שַׂר: The consuming teeth (Shin) at the head (Resh) — the devouring fire that stands at the top, the prince who rules by the sharpness of his authority.
תָּוֶךְ: The covenant mark (Tav) hooked (Vav) and bent by the open palm (Kaph) — a sign pinned in place and cupped by the yielding hand, the middle point where two halves meet.
חֶרֶב: The fence (Chet) of the chief (Resh) inside the house (Beth) — the barrier that separates at the head of the dwelling, the sword as a wall of sharp division within.
שָׁאוּל: The consuming teeth (Shin) of the ox-strength (Aleph) hooked (Vav) to the goad (Lamed) — primal fire and power fastened to the directing rod, the one who was asked for and demanded from above.
כֶּסֶף: The open palm (Kaph) props up (Samech) what the mouth (Pe) speaks — the yielded hand that supports the word, silver as the value that backs the promise and bends to the spoken price.
נָא: The living fish (Nun) turns to the ox-strength (Aleph) — life swimming toward the strong one, the earnest plea of the small creature seeking the mighty, I pray.
מִזְבֵּחַ: The mighty waters (Mem) cut by the weapon (Zayin) inside the house (Beth) of the fence (Chet) — the place of slaughter within the walled enclosure, the altar where chaos is severed and contained.
יָם: The hand (Yod) of the waters (Mem) — the working deed plunged into the mighty chaos, the sea as a vast hand of surging water.
שֶׁבַע: The consuming teeth (Shin) inside the house (Beth) seen by the eye (Ayin) — fire dwelling within the home and perceived, seven as the fullness that is witnessed within the complete dwelling.
זָהָב: The cutting weapon (Zayin) revealed through the window (He) inside the house (Beth) — the gleaming metal seen through the opening, gold as the shining blade kept in the treasury.
יָרַד: The hand (Yod) of the chief (Resh) entering the door (Dalet) — the head going down through the threshold, the descent of the leader passing through the low doorway.
רוּחַ: The head/chief (Resh) hooked (Vav) through the fence (Chet) — the breath that crosses the boundary, the wind as an invisible force pushing past every wall.
אֵשׁ: The ox-strength (Aleph) consumed by teeth (Shin) — the primal power devoured and released, fire as raw strength unleashed through destruction.
נְאֻם: The living fish (Nun) of the ox-strength (Aleph) in the waters (Mem) — life from the mighty one surging up from the deep, the oracle as a living word rising from the source.
בָּנָה: The house (Beth) of the living fish (Nun) revealed by breath (He) — life growing inside the dwelling until it breathes and is seen, building as the act of making a home for the living.
שַׁעַר: The consuming teeth (Shin) of the eye (Ayin) at the head (Resh) — the devouring gaze at the chief place, the gate as the sharp-eyed opening where the city watches and judges.
נָגַד: The living fish (Nun) carried by the camel (Gimel) through the door (Dalet) — the active life lifted up and brought through the threshold, the declaration that carries a message forward into the open.
דָּם: The door (Dalet) of the waters (Mem) — the threshold where the mighty flow pours through, blood as the red tide that passes the gateway of the body.
אָנֹכִי: The ox-strength (Aleph) of the living fish (Nun) yielded by the open palm (Kaph) and worked by the hand (Yod) — the strong living self, held open and active, the I that stands with its own power in its own hands.
יַעֲקֹב: The hand (Yod) sees (Ayin) the sun on the horizon (Qoph) from the house (Beth) — the working hand that watches and follows from inside the dwelling, the heel-grasper who saw destiny and reached for it.
מָלַךְ: The mighty waters (Mem) goaded (Lamed) by the open palm (Kaph) — the chaos directed by the yielding hand, the king who rules the flood with an open palm that both commands and restrains.
אַהֲרוֹן: The ox-strength (Aleph) revealed through the window (He) at the chief (Resh), hooked (Vav) to life (Nun) — the strong one who breathes and stands at the head, connected to the living, Aaron the high priest.
אֹהֶל: The ox-strength (Aleph) breathing through the window (He), directed by the goad (Lamed) — the strong breath that billows under the rod's guidance, the tent as a shelter of wind and hide stretched by poles.
דִּי: The door (Dalet) worked by the hand (Yod) — the threshold that the hand opens and closes, the relative pronoun that points through to what follows.
חָמֵשׁ: The fence (Chet) holding back the waters (Mem) consumed by teeth (Shin) — the walled enclosure containing the mighty fire, five as the closed hand that grips and guards.
עָשָׂר: The eye (Ayin) of the consuming teeth (Shin) at the head (Resh) — the perceiving fire at the chief point, ten as the full count seen at the top.
סָבִיב: The support (Samech) of the house (Beth) worked by the hand (Yod) in the house (Beth) again — one dwelling propped up against the next, the circle of support that surrounds and returns to itself.
בָּרַךְ: The house (Beth) of the chief (Resh) yielding the open palm (Kaph) — the dwelling of the head bowing down with hands outstretched, kneeling to bless with the palms turned upward.
יָרֵא: The hand (Yod) of the chief (Resh) before the ox-strength (Aleph) — the working hand that trembles at the sight of the mighty leader, fear as the awe when the small stands before the great.
אָדוֹן: The ox-strength (Aleph) at the door (Dalet) hooked (Vav) to life (Nun) — the strong one standing at the threshold, connected to all living things, the sovereign master of the household.
כְּלִי: The open palm (Kaph) goaded (Lamed) by the hand (Yod) — the yielding hand shaped and directed by the working hand, the vessel as something bent and formed by labor for a purpose.
שָׂדֶה: The consuming teeth (Shin) opening the door (Dalet) to the breath (He) — fire breaking through the threshold into the open air, the field as the wide place where sun burns and wind blows.
אוֹ: The ox-strength (Aleph) hooked (Vav) — the primal power connecting to another option, desire as the link between one possibility and the next.
מִלְחָמָה: The mighty waters (Mem) goaded (Lamed) through the fence (Chet), flooding (Mem) and revealed (He) — chaos directed against the wall, breaking through the barrier for all to see, the battle laid bare.
אַרְבַּע: The ox-strength (Aleph) at the head (Resh) inside the house (Beth) seen by the eye (Ayin) — the strong chief in the dwelling perceived from every direction, four as the corners the eye sees from the center.
הֵן: The breath/behold (He) of the living fish (Nun) — the sudden revelation of teeming life, lo! the gasp of seeing something alive and moving.
נָבִיא: The living fish (Nun) inside the house (Beth), worked by the hand (Yod) of the ox-strength (Aleph) — life dwelling within, shaped by the mighty hand, the prophet as one through whom the strong one works from inside.
עֶשְׂרִים: The eye (Ayin) of the consuming teeth (Shin) at the head (Resh), worked by the hand (Yod) in the waters (Mem) — the fiery gaze of the chief laboring in the deep, twenty as two tens counted across the mighty expanse.
עָנָה: The eye (Ayin) of the living fish (Nun) revealed by breath (He) — the seeing life that breathes a response, the answer that rises from the depths of perception into the open air.
יְהֹוִה: The hand (Yod) breathes (He) and hooks (Vav) to breath again (He) — the working deed that reveals, connects, and reveals once more, the self-existing One whose every act is living breath.
רַע: The head (Resh) of the eye (Ayin) — the chief perception turned inward, the seeing that spoils what it looks upon, evil as sight bent on itself.
מִשְׁפָּחָה: The mighty waters (Mem) consume with teeth (Shin), speak through the mouth (Pe), are enclosed by the fence (Chet) and revealed (He) — the bloodline that devours, speaks, is bounded, and brought to light, the family as a walled clan with one voice.
פָּקַד: The mouth (Pe) follows the sun (Qoph) through the door (Dalet) — the spoken word that traces the horizon and enters the threshold, the visit as the authority that comes to inspect and account.
סוּר: The support (Samech) hooks (Vav) away from the head (Resh) — the prop that disconnects from the chief, the turning aside as the pillar that unhooks and withdraws from its place.
לֶחֶם: The goad (Lamed) drives through the fence (Chet) into the waters (Mem) — the rod that pushes past the barrier into the nourishing deep, bread as sustenance wrested from the enclosed earth.
עֵת: The eye (Ayin) marked by the covenant (Tav) — the moment perceived and sealed, time as the appointed instant that the eye sees and the mark records.
שְׁלֹמֹה: The consuming teeth (Shin) guided by the goad (Lamed) over the waters (Mem) and revealed by breath (He) — fire directed across the deep and made visible, Solomon as the peace that comes when the burning is governed and shown.
חַטָּאָה: The fence (Chet) holds the coiling serpent (Tet) and the ox-strength (Aleph), revealed by breath (He) — the snake coiled around the strong one inside the enclosure, sin as the hidden trap laid bare.
חָזַק: The fence (Chet) armed with the weapon (Zayin) follows the sun (Qoph) — the barrier fortified with the cutting blade that pursues the horizon, strength as the armed wall that never yields.
כָּרַת: The open palm (Kaph) of the chief (Resh) sealed by the covenant mark (Tav) — the yielding hand of the head making the final sign, to cut a covenant by dividing and marking.
עָבַד: The eye (Ayin) inside the house (Beth) entering the door (Dalet) — the one who sees from within the dwelling and passes through the threshold to labor, work as the servant going out from the master's house.
פְּלִשְׁתִּי: The mouth (Pe) goads (Lamed) with consuming teeth (Shin), sealed by the covenant mark (Tav) and worked by the hand (Yod) — the edge that drives and devours under a foreign sign, the Philistine as the invading voice that claims with fire.
עֹלָה: The eye (Ayin) goaded upward (Lamed) and revealed by breath (He) — what is seen rising under the rod's direction and made visible, the burnt offering that ascends as smoke into the open air.
צָבָא: The fish hook (Tsade) seizes the house (Beth) of the ox-strength (Aleph) — the host is the desire that catches hold of the strong dwelling, the army mustered and drawn together for war.
לֵוִיִּי: The goad (Lamed) hooks (Vav) the hand (Yod) to another hand (Yod) — the directing rod that connects one worker to the next, the Levite as one joined and bound to sacred service.
בְּרִית: The house (Beth) of the chief (Resh) worked by the hand (Yod) and sealed with the covenant mark (Tav) — the dwelling of the head where the deed is done and the sign is cut, the covenant as a bond forged inside the leader's house.
חֹדֶשׁ: The fence (Chet) opens the door (Dalet) to the consuming teeth (Shin) — the enclosed darkness that passes through the threshold into burning light, the new moon as renewal emerging from the hidden place.
קָרַב: The sun on the horizon (Qoph) at the head (Resh) of the house (Beth) — the rising light drawing near to the chief dwelling, the approach as the coming closer of what was distant.
אֹיֵב: The ox-strength (Aleph) worked by the hand (Yod) inside the house (Beth) — the strong one whose hand operates within your dwelling, the enemy as the hostile power that has entered your home.
אַף: The ox-strength (Aleph) at the mouth (Pe) — the primal force speaking at the edge, the addition that comes with power, also, even, moreover.
צֹאן: The fish hook (Tsade) of the ox-strength (Aleph) with the living fish (Nun) — the desire that gathers the strong and the living together, the flock as the drawn-in and kept multitude.
אֶבֶן: The ox-strength (Aleph) inside the house (Beth) of the living fish (Nun) — the strong foundation within the dwelling of life, the stone as the enduring power that holds the house together.
מַעַן: The waters (Mem) of the eye (Ayin) with the living fish (Nun) — the mighty gaze that follows the living, the purpose that watches and moves with intent toward its object.
מִדְבָּר: The mighty waters (Mem) at the door (Dalet) of the house (Beth) of the chief (Resh) — the chaos at the threshold of the leader's dwelling, the wilderness as the vast untamed place beyond the last house.
בָּשָׂר: The house (Beth) consumed by teeth (Shin) at the head (Resh) — the dwelling devoured from the top down, flesh as the body that can be eaten, the house of the self stripped bare.
פַּרְעֹה: The mouth (Pe) of the chief (Resh) seen by the eye (Ayin) and revealed (He) — the speaking head perceived and made visible, Pharaoh as the great house whose commanding mouth is beheld by all.
חָיָה: The fence (Chet) worked by the hand (Yod) and revealed by breath (He) — life as what the hand brings forth from the enclosed space into the breathing world, to live as the act of emerging.
בָּבֶל: The house (Beth) upon the house (Beth) goaded (Lamed) — one dwelling stacked on another and driven upward, Babel as the confusion of houses piled toward heaven under the goad of ambition.
רָשָׁע: The head (Resh) consumed by teeth (Shin) in the eye (Ayin) — the chief devoured by fire in plain sight, wickedness as the destruction that everyone can see at the top.
לֵבָב: The goad (Lamed) inside the house (Beth) and inside the house (Beth) again — the directing rod dwelling deep within the innermost room, the heart as the doubled interior where all intent is driven.
מַטֶּה: The mighty waters (Mem) coiled by the serpent (Tet) and revealed by breath (He) — the chaos bound by the snake and made visible, the rod or staff as the contained power shown forth, the branch of a tribe.
מָלֵא: The mighty waters (Mem) goaded (Lamed) toward the ox-strength (Aleph) — the flood driven toward the source until nothing is empty, to fill as the great waters directed until they overflow with strength.
רֶגֶל: The head (Resh) carried by the camel (Gimel) and guided by the goad (Lamed) — the chief lifted and driven forward step by step, the foot as the bearer that carries and is directed on the journey.
אַמָּה: The ox-strength (Aleph) of the waters (Mem) revealed by breath (He) — the strong measure rising from the deep and made visible, the cubit as the mother-measure from which all building begins.
חֶסֶד: The fence (Chet) propped up (Samech) at the door (Dalet) — the enclosure supported and opened at the threshold, lovingkindness as the sheltering wall that holds you up and keeps the way open.
חַי: The fence/inner room (Chet) worked by the hand (Yod) — life as the active deed inside the bounded space, the pulse of work behind the wall that proves something is alive.
גְּבוּל: The camel (Gimel) carries goods to the house (Beth), pegged down (Vav) and directed by the goad (Lamed) — the boundary is where the loaded journey stops, the marked limit of the dwelling's reach.
נַעַר: The living fish (Nun) sees with fresh eyes (Ayin) under a new head (Resh) — a boy is the darting life that perceives everything for the first time, the young chief not yet grown.
חָטָא: The fence (Chet) coils with the snake (Tet) around the ox-strength (Aleph) — sin as missing the mark, the barrier and the twisting serpent that entangle the strong one and pull him off course.
שָׁלוֹם: The consuming fire (Shin) is directed by the goad (Lamed), pegged securely (Vav) over the waters (Mem) — peace as the fierce energy taught and fastened above the chaos, wholeness that tames the deep.
מַעֲשֶׂה: The mighty waters (Mem) seen by the eye (Ayin), consumed by fire (Shin), revealed through the window (He) — a deed is what is perceived, pressed through effort, and brought into the open.
חַיִל: The inner room (Chet) where the hand (Yod) is goaded to action (Lamed) — force is the strength walled inside, driven outward by discipline, the army trained behind the fence.
לַיִל: The goad (Lamed) directs the hand (Yod), then the goad returns (Lamed) — night is the twisting cycle where one direction works and turns back again, darkness wheeling around.
זָכַר: The weapon (Zayin) cuts into the open palm (Kaph) of the chief (Resh) — to remember is to score a mark into the hand of the head, an incision that cannot be forgotten.
יָרַשׁ: The hand (Yod) of the chief (Resh) devours with teeth (Shin) — to possess is the working hand of the head consuming all rivals, seizing the inheritance by force.
רָבָה: The chief (Resh) fills the house (Beth) until it breathes open (He) — to increase is the head multiplying within the dwelling until it must open its windows to contain the overflow.
עָוֺן: The eye (Ayin) hooked (Vav) to the living fish (Nun) — iniquity is the twisted gaze fastened to restless activity, the seeing that is bent and attached to wrong.
קֶרֶב: The sun on the horizon (Qoph) follows the chief (Resh) into the house (Beth) — the nearest part is the inner dwelling where the head comes home at dusk, the midst of things.
בָּקַשׁ: The house (Beth) chases the sun on the horizon (Qoph) with consuming teeth (Shin) — to search is the dwelling pursuing the vanishing light with fierce hunger, seeking what lies beyond.
אֲדָמָה: The ox-strength (Aleph) enters the door (Dalet) into the waters (Mem) revealed by breath (He) — the soil is the strong earth opened by the pathway, watered and made visible, the ground from which life breathes.
כָּתַב: The open palm (Kaph) presses a covenant mark (Tav) inside the house (Beth) — to write is the hand engraving a sign into the interior of the dwelling, the permanent inscription.
מוֹעֵד: The mighty waters (Mem) hooked (Vav) to the watching eye (Ayin) at the door (Dalet) — an appointed time is the powerful current fastened to the moment the eye meets the threshold, the scheduled gathering.
נַחֲלָה: The living fish (Nun) within the fence (Chet), directed by the goad (Lamed) and revealed (He) — an inheritance is the active life enclosed and passed down by teaching, made visible across generations.
אֵם: The ox-strength (Aleph) of the waters (Mem) — the mother is the mighty source, the primal strength flowing like water, the powerful origin of all life.
תּוֹרָה: The covenant mark (Tav) hooked (Vav) to the chief (Resh), revealed through the window (He) — the Torah is the sign fastened to the head and made visible, the instruction that connects the mark to understanding.
יְהוֹשׁוּעַ: The hand (Yod) reveals breath (He), hooks (Vav) through consuming fire (Shin), connects (Vav) to the seeing eye (Ayin) — Joshua is the working hand of the revealed God that devours and rescues what it sees.
שָׁתָה: The teeth (Shin) press down to the covenant mark (Tav), breathing in (He) — to drink is the consuming mouth sealed to the cup, drawing breath inward with every swallow.
כּוּן: The open palm (Kaph) pegged firmly (Vav) to the living fish (Nun) — to be established is the yielding hand fastened securely to active life, the prepared and stable foundation.
מַחֲנֶה: The mighty waters (Mem) within the fence (Chet), alive with fish (Nun) and revealed (He) — an encampment is the mighty host fenced in, teeming with life behind the walls, visible from afar.
בֶּגֶד: The house (Beth) carried by the camel (Gimel) through the door (Dalet) — a garment is what the dwelling lifts and carries through the threshold, the covering that travels with you.
שֵׁשׁ: Teeth (Shin) consuming teeth (Shin) — six is the doubled fire, the complete devouring that marks the day of man's labor before the rest.
עָזַב: The eye (Ayin) cuts with the weapon (Zayin) from the house (Beth) — to forsake is the seeing that severs itself from the dwelling, the gaze that turns away and cuts the bond of home.
בֹּקֶר: The house (Beth) follows the sun on the horizon (Qoph) toward the chief (Resh) — morning is the dwelling chasing the rising light to the head, the break of day when the first light reaches home.
נָטָה: The living fish (Nun) coils like a snake (Tet) and is revealed (He) — to stretch is the active life extending and bending outward, made visible as it reaches and inclines.
נָצַל: The living fish (Nun) caught by the fish hook (Tsade) and driven by the goad (Lamed) — to snatch away is the life seized on the hook and goaded free, the rescue that pulls the living from danger.
מַלְאָךְ: The mighty waters (Mem) goaded (Lamed) by ox-strength (Aleph), yielding the open palm (Kaph) — a messenger is the powerful one directed by the first authority, who arrives with open hands to deliver the word.
יוֹסֵף: The hand (Yod) hooks (Vav) to the prop (Samech) and speaks (Pe) — Joseph is the working hand that connects to support and adds by the word of his mouth, the one who increases.
שָׁכַב: The consuming teeth (Shin) yield to the open palm (Kaph) inside the house (Beth) — to lie down is the fire surrendering to the resting hand within the dwelling, the body pressing flat in the home.
מִנְחָה: The mighty waters (Mem) of the living fish (Nun) brought to the fence (Chet) and revealed (He) — an offering is the life drawn from the deep, brought to the sacred enclosure, and presented in the open.
יָסַף: The hand (Yod) props up (Samech) what the mouth (Pe) has spoken — to add is the working hand supporting the word, building more upon what was already said.
אָהַב: The ox-strength (Aleph) breathes (He) into the house (Beth) — to love is the first and strongest power breathing life into the dwelling, the mighty one filling the home with his presence.
יָשַׁע: The hand (Yod) devours the fire (Shin) so the eye (Ayin) can see — to save is the working hand consuming the destruction so that sight is restored, deliverance as clearing the way for vision.
צַדִּיק: The fish hook (Tsade) enters the door (Dalet), worked by the hand (Yod) toward the horizon (Qoph) — the righteous one is the desire that passes through the threshold, laboring toward the light.
כָּלָה: The open palm (Kaph) goaded (Lamed) until it breathes its last (He) — to end is the yielding hand driven forward until nothing remains but a sigh, the completion that empties everything out.
אָסַף: The ox-strength (Aleph) props up (Samech) the mouth (Pe) — to gather is the strong one surrounding and supporting what speaks, collecting everything into one sheltered place.
שָׁפַט: The consuming teeth (Shin) of the speaking mouth (Pe) surrounded by the snake (Tet) — to judge is the devouring word that encircles and contains, the sharp speech that coils around the case.
אָרוֹן: The ox-strength (Aleph) at the chief (Resh), hooked (Vav) to the living fish (Nun) — the ark is the primal power of the head fastened to life, the strong box that preserves what lives.
בַּד: The house (Beth) at the door (Dalet) — separation is the space between the dwelling and the threshold, the lone branch standing apart at the entry.
כָּבוֹד: The open palm (Kaph) of the house (Beth) hooked (Vav) to the door (Dalet) — glory is the weight of the yielding household fastened to the doorway, the heavy presence that fills the entire entrance.
שֶׁמֶן: The consuming teeth (Shin) of the waters (Mem) and the living fish (Nun) — oil is what the fire presses from the mighty flow of life, the grease that rises when substance is crushed.
יָכֹל: The hand (Yod) yields the palm (Kaph) to the goad (Lamed) — to be able is the working hand that opens and submits to direction, the power that comes from disciplined yielding.
כַּף: The open palm (Kaph) at the mouth (Pe) — the hollow hand is the yielding cup at the edge of speech, the sole and palm that curves to receive or give.
שֵׁבֶט: The teeth (Shin) of the house (Beth) coiled by the snake (Tet) — the rod is the consuming authority of the household that encircles and contains, the scepter of the tribe.
בְּהֵמָה: The house (Beth) breathes (He) over the waters (Mem) and breathes again (He) — a beast is the dwelling of breath upon the mighty deep, the dumb creature whose life is only breathing, breathing.
רֵעַ: The chief (Resh) seen by the eye (Ayin) — a friend is the one whose face the head turns to see, the companion who stands in your line of sight.
רוּם: The head (Resh) hooked (Vav) above the waters (Mem) — to be high is the chief fastened above the mighty deep, the exalted one lifted over the chaos below.
אֹזֶן: The ox-strength (Aleph) cut by the weapon (Zayin) to the living fish (Nun) — the ear is the strong opening carved to catch the darting movement of life, the broadness that receives every sound.
גָּלָה: The camel (Gimel) goaded (Lamed) until revealed (He) — to uncover is the loaded beast driven forward until everything it carries is laid bare in the open window.
שָׁבַע: The consuming teeth (Shin) of the house (Beth) seeing the eye (Ayin) — to swear is the fire of the household witnessed by the eye, the oath taken under the devouring gaze of seven.
סֵפֶר: The prop (Samech) of the mouth (Pe) at the head (Resh) — a scroll is the support that upholds speech to the chief, the writing that preserves the spoken word for the leader.
אָבַד: The ox-strength (Aleph) leaves the house (Beth) through the door (Dalet) — to perish is the strong one walking out of the dwelling through the exit, the power that wanders away and is lost.
בָּקָר: The house (Beth) follows the sun (Qoph) toward the chief (Resh) — cattle are the herd of the household that follows the dawn toward the head, the beeves that rise and move with the morning light.
רִאשׁוֹן: The head (Resh) of ox-strength (Aleph) consumed by fire (Shin), hooked (Vav) to life (Nun) — the first is the chief and the strong one pressed through fire and fastened to the living, the foremost in everything.
יַרְדֵּן: The hand (Yod) of the chief (Resh) enters the door (Dalet) of the living fish (Nun) — the Jordan is the working descent of the head through the threshold into the teeming waters, the river that flows down to life.
מוֹאָב: The mighty waters (Mem) hooked (Vav) to the ox-strength (Aleph) in the house (Beth) — Moab is the powerful stream connected to the strong one's dwelling, the people born from the father's house.
מִצְוָה: The mighty waters (Mem) caught by the fish hook (Tsade), connected (Vav) and revealed (He) — a commandment is the powerful desire hooked and fastened, then brought to light for all to see.
אֶפְרַיִם: The ox-strength (Aleph) speaks (Pe) from the head (Resh), worked by the hand (Yod) in mighty waters (Mem) — Ephraim is the doubly fruitful one, the strong mouth of the chief laboring over the abundant deep.
זָקֵן: The weapon (Zayin) follows the sun on the horizon (Qoph) to the living fish (Nun) — the elder is the one who has cut through many sunsets and still sustains life, the old one seasoned by time.
עֶשֶׂר: The eye (Ayin) of the consuming teeth (Shin) at the chief (Resh) — ten is the full count perceived by the fire at the head, the complete set seen and devoured by the leading number.
שָׂפָה: The teeth (Shin) of the mouth (Pe) revealed (He) — the lip is where consuming speech is made visible, the edge of the mouth opened for all to behold.
אַבְרָהָם: The ox-strength (Aleph) of the house (Beth) at the chief (Resh), revealed by breath (He) over the waters (Mem) — Abraham is the strong father of the household whose headship is breathed out over a multitude like the sea.
שָׁאַל: The consuming teeth (Shin) of the ox-strength (Aleph) goaded (Lamed) — to inquire is the fire of the strong one driven forward by the prod, the burning question directed toward its answer.
קָדַשׁ: The sun on the horizon (Qoph) enters the door (Dalet) and is consumed by fire (Shin) — to be holy is what comes through the threshold from the horizon and is devoured, set apart by the burning.
שְׁלוֹשִׁים: The consuming teeth (Shin) goaded (Lamed) and hooked (Vav), then teeth again (Shin) worked by the hand (Yod) in the waters (Mem) — thirty is the triple fire directed and fastened, multiplied by the laboring hand in the mighty deep.
שָׁחָה: The consuming teeth (Shin) press into the inner room (Chet) until the breath escapes (He) — to bow down is the fire of the self driven into the enclosed space, crushed low until only a sigh remains.
לָחַם: The goad (Lamed) drives through the fence (Chet) into the waters (Mem) — to devour is the directed thrust through the barrier into the mighty deep, the feeding and fighting that pushes through walls.
בִּין: The house (Beth) where the hand (Yod) of the living fish (Nun) works — to discern is the dwelling place where the active hand separates the living from the dead, understanding built between two things.
בָּחַר: The house (Beth) behind the fence (Chet) of the chief (Resh) — to choose is to enter the enclosed place of the head, testing what lies within the barrier before selecting the best.
הָרַג: Behold (He) the head (Resh) carried off by the camel (Gimel) — to slay is the revealed chief lifted and carried away, the killing that removes the head from its place.
דּוֹר: The door (Dalet) hooked (Vav) to the chief (Resh) — a generation is the threshold connected to the head, one doorway fastened to the next leader, the turning of ages from father to son.
מְלָאכָה: The mighty waters (Mem) goaded (Lamed) by ox-strength (Aleph), yielding the palm (Kaph) revealed (He) — work is the powerful labor directed by the strong one, its product held in the open hand for all to see.
אַחֵר: The ox-strength (Aleph) on the far side of the fence (Chet), at a different head (Resh) — another is the strong one separated by the barrier, the chief who stands behind the wall.
בִּנְיָמִין: The house (Beth) of the living fish (Nun), worked by the hand (Yod) in the mighty waters (Mem), worked again (Yod) as a living fish (Nun) — Benjamin is the son of the right hand, the household of darting life laboring in the deep, doubled in vitality.
פֶּתַח: The mouth (Pe) at the covenant mark (Tav) of the fence (Chet) — an opening is where speech meets the sign at the barrier, the entrance that declares itself at the wall's edge.
חוּץ: The fence (Chet) hooks outward (Vav) to the fish hook (Tsade) — the outside is what connects beyond the wall, the hook that reaches past the barrier to pull toward the open field.
דָּרַשׁ: The door (Dalet) of the chief (Resh) consumed by fire (Shin) — to seek is to press through the threshold of the head with burning intensity, the inquiry that treads fiercely after its answer.
חֲמִשִּׁים: The fence (Chet) of the mighty waters (Mem) consumed by fire (Shin), worked by the hand (Yod) in the waters again (Mem) — fifty is the walled enclosure of the deep burned and labored over, the jubilee number pressed from chaos.
זֶבַח: The weapon (Zayin) inside the house (Beth) at the fence (Chet) — a sacrifice is the blade brought into the dwelling at the sacred enclosure, the cutting within the walled holy place.
אַךְ: The ox-strength (Aleph) yielding the open palm (Kaph) — surely, the strong one opens his hand in affirmation, the powerful gesture of certain assent.
טָמֵא: The coiled snake (Tet) in the waters (Mem) back to the ox-strength (Aleph) — the serpent wrapped in the chaos returning to corrupt the first, unclean as the contamination that circles back to the source.
נוּס: The living fish (Nun) hooks away (Vav) from the prop (Samech) — to flee is the darting life that unhooks from its support and darts away, the fish that breaks free and vanishes.
גִּבּוֹר: The camel (Gimel) inside the house (Beth), hooked (Vav) to the chief (Resh) — a mighty man is the one who carries the burden of the dwelling and is fastened to the head, the champion who lifts and leads.
מָוֶת: The mighty waters (Mem) hooked (Vav) to the covenant mark (Tav) — death is the great deep fastened to its final sign, the flood connected to the last seal.
סָבַב: The prop (Samech) surrounding the house (Beth) within a house (Beth) — to revolve is the encircling support around dwelling after dwelling, the turning that wraps around and comes back to where it started.
צְדָקָה: The fish hook (Tsade) enters the door (Dalet), follows the sun (Qoph), and is revealed (He) — righteousness is the desire that passes through the threshold toward the horizon and is made visible, justice brought to light.
שֵׁנִי: The teeth (Shin) of the living fish (Nun) worked by the hand (Yod) — the second is the consuming life that works again, the repeat of the first devouring cycle under the working hand.
רָעָה: The chief (Resh) sees with the eye (Ayin) and reveals through breath (He) — to shepherd is the head watching over with open eyes and breathing out guidance, the tending gaze of the one in charge.
שָׂמַח: The consuming teeth (Shin) of the mighty waters (Mem) break against the fence (Chet) — joy is the fierce surge crashing into the barrier and splashing upward, the brightening that comes when deep waters meet the wall.
אַיִל: The ox-strength (Aleph) worked by the hand (Yod) toward the goad (Lamed) — the ram is the mighty one driven forward by the working hand, the powerful beast directed with purpose.
צִיּוֹן: The fish hook (Tsade) worked by the hand (Yod), pegged (Vav) to the living fish (Nun) — Zion is the righteous desire labored by the hand and fastened to enduring life, the holy mount anchored forever.
צָפוֹן: The fish hook (Tsade) speaks (Pe), connected (Vav) to the living fish (Nun) — the north is the hidden direction where the desire whispers and hooks into the unseen life, the concealed region beyond the edge.
כָּסָה: The open palm (Kaph) props up (Samech) and conceals until revealed (He) — to cover is the yielding hand surrounding and hiding something, then breathing over it, the cloak that both hides and shelters.
אַשּׁוּר: The ox-strength (Aleph) consumed by fire (Shin), hooked (Vav) to the chief (Resh) — Assyria is the fierce power devoured by its own flame and fastened to the head, the strong empire of consuming might.
הָלַל: Behold (He) the goad (Lamed) driving toward the goad again (Lamed) — the breath that shines through and directs again and again, the radiance of repeated praise.
נָגַע: The living fish (Nun) carried by the camel (Gimel) to the eye (Ayin) — life lifted and brought near until it is seen and felt, the moment of touch.
נֶגֶד: The living fish (Nun) carried by the camel (Gimel) to the door (Dalet) — life lifted up and set before the entrance, placed in full view, the front.
עֵדָה: The eye (Ayin) sees through the door (Dalet) and breathes (He) — those who are witnessed passing through the same gate and sharing the same breath, an assembly.
רֹב: The head (Resh) filling the house (Beth) — the chief whose household overflows, abundance spilling from every room of the dwelling.
חׇכְמָה: The fence (Chet) yields the open palm (Kaph) over the waters (Mem), revealed by breath (He) — the inner room that opens its hand over chaos and brings order into view, wisdom.
שָׁבַר: The teeth (Shin) consume the house (Beth) of the chief (Resh) — fire devouring the dwelling from the top down, the shattering burst that leaves nothing standing.
שָׁחַת: The teeth (Shin) consume the fence (Chet) marked by the covenant (Tav) — fire eating through the wall and defacing the sacred sign, decay and corruption.
יִרְמְיָה: The hand (Yod) of the chief (Resh) over the waters (Mem), worked by the hand (Yod) and revealed by breath (He) — the one whom the LORD hurls forth, Jeremiah.
שָׂנֵא: The teeth (Shin) devour the living fish (Nun) back to the ox-strength (Aleph) — the consuming fire that destroys life at its source, the burning of hate.
נָסַע: The living fish (Nun) propped up (Samech) and seen by the eye (Ayin) — life supported and set in motion under the watching eye, pulling up stakes to journey onward.
מְנַשֶּׁה: The waters (Mem) of the living fish (Nun) consumed by fire (Shin) and revealed by breath (He) — the mighty life that forgets its consuming past, Manasseh, the one who causes to forget.
חָצֵר: The fence (Chet) hooks the desire (Tsade) of the chief (Resh) — the walled enclosure that catches and holds the leader's longing, the courtyard.
עֲבֹדָה: The eye (Ayin) sees inside the house (Beth) through the door (Dalet) revealed by breath (He) — the labor witnessed at the threshold of the dwelling, work made visible.
יוֹאָב: The hand (Yod) connected (Vav) to ox-strength (Aleph) inside the house (Beth) — the working deed hooked to the strong father's dwelling, Joab, whose father is the LORD.
רָדַף: The head (Resh) enters the door (Dalet) with the mouth (Pe) crying out — the chief bursting through the gateway shouting, the relentless pursuit that will not stop.
חָנָה: The fence (Chet) shelters the living fish (Nun) revealed by breath (He) — life enclosed and breathing within the wall, pitching camp and inclining to rest.
כְּמוֹ: The open palm (Kaph) over the waters (Mem) hooked (Vav) — the yielding hand reaching across the deep and connecting, the comparison that bridges two things, as.
יַיִן: The hand (Yod) works and the hand (Yod) works again upon the living fish (Nun) — double labor pressing the life from the grape, the crushed fruit becoming wine.
מַעַל: The waters (Mem) of the eye (Ayin) directed by the goad (Lamed) — the mighty seeing aimed upward, the perception that rises above, the upper part.
נַחַל: The living fish (Nun) inside the fence (Chet) driven by the goad (Lamed) — the swimming life channeled between walls and directed forward, a rushing stream.
שְׁמוּאֵל: The teeth (Shin) over the waters (Mem) connected (Vav) to ox-strength (Aleph) guided by the goad (Lamed) — the consuming name over the deep, hooked to God's power and directed, Samuel, heard of God.
יָמִין: The hand (Yod) over the waters (Mem) works (Yod) upon the living fish (Nun) — the strong hand that masters the mighty deep and grasps the life within, the right hand of power.
נְחֹשֶׁת: The living fish (Nun) inside the fence (Chet) consumed by fire (Shin) and marked by the covenant (Tav) — life walled in, smelted by flame, and stamped with a sign, copper forged and sealed.
מִשְׁכָּן: The waters (Mem) consumed by fire (Shin) yield the open palm (Kaph) to the living fish (Nun) — the mighty flame that opens its hand and shelters life within, the tabernacle dwelling.
יֵשׁ: The hand (Yod) consumed by fire (Shin) — the working deed pressed through the flame and proven real, the substance that exists.
סוּס: The prop (Samech) connected (Vav) to the prop (Samech) — support hooked to support, the strong-legged beast that carries and upholds on every side, the horse.
חָכָם: The fence (Chet) yields the open palm (Kaph) over the waters (Mem) — the one who dwells inside the wall and opens his hand to master the chaos, the wise.
פָּנָה: The mouth (Pe) of the living fish (Nun) revealed by breath (He) — the speaking life that turns toward the window, the face turning to behold.
כִּסֵּא: The open palm (Kaph) propped and supported (Samech) by ox-strength (Aleph) — the yielding hand upheld by the strongest power, the covered seat, the throne.
פָּתַח: The mouth (Pe) marks the covenant (Tav) at the fence (Chet) — the speaking edge breaks the seal on the inner wall, opening wide what was shut.
אַרְבָּעִים: The ox-strength (Aleph) of the chief (Resh) inside the house (Beth) seen by the eye (Ayin), worked by the hand (Yod) over the waters (Mem) — the fullness of strength tested and proven through every trial, forty.
מִסְפָּר: The waters (Mem) supported (Samech) by the mouth (Pe) of the chief (Resh) — the mighty deep held in order by the leader's spoken count, the reckoning of number.
זָבַח: The weapon (Zayin) cuts inside the house (Beth) within the fence (Chet) — the blade falling in the enclosed dwelling, the act of slaughter for sacrifice.
גִּלְעָד: The camel (Gimel) goaded (Lamed) toward the eye (Ayin) at the door (Dalet) — the one who carries and drives toward the witness at the threshold, Gilead, the heap of witness.
חָלַל: The fence (Chet) pierced by the goad (Lamed) and the goad again (Lamed) — the inner wall bored through twice, the profaned boundary, the pierced and wounded one.
שֶׁמֶשׁ: The teeth (Shin) consuming the waters (Mem) consumed by teeth (Shin) again — fire devouring the deep and flaring back, the blaze that rises and sets, the sun.
פֵּן: The mouth (Pe) of the living fish (Nun) — the word that swims away from life, the spoken warning that removes, lest.
קָבַר: The sun on the horizon (Qoph) enters the house (Beth) of the chief (Resh) — the light following the leader into the dwelling beneath the earth, burial.
אֲרָם: The ox-strength (Aleph) of the chief (Resh) over the waters (Mem) — the mighty leader elevated above the deep, Aram, the highland between the rivers.
פַּר: The mouth (Pe) of the head (Resh) — the bellowing chief, the young bull whose voice leads the herd.
חוֹמָה: The fence (Chet) connected (Vav) to the waters (Mem) revealed by breath (He) — the barrier hooked to the mighty flood and standing visible, the wall that holds back the deep.
שָׁאַר: The teeth (Shin) of the ox-strength (Aleph) at the head (Resh) — what the consuming fire leaves of the strong chief, the remnant that remains.
חִזְקִיָּה: The fence (Chet) armed with the weapon (Zayin) follows the sun (Qoph), worked by the hand (Yod) revealed by breath (He) — the walled strength that takes up arms and endures, Hezekiah, the LORD strengthens.
נָשִׂיא: The living fish (Nun) consumed by fire (Shin), worked by the hand (Yod) of ox-strength (Aleph) — the life that passes through the flame and is lifted by the mighty hand, the exalted prince.
שָׁכַן: The teeth (Shin) yield the open palm (Kaph) to the living fish (Nun) — the consuming fire that opens its hand and shelters life, to settle down and reside.
קָבַץ: The sun on the horizon (Qoph) enters the house (Beth) and is caught by the fish hook (Tsade) — the scattered light drawn in and gathered at the dwelling, to grasp together.
אֶמֶת: The ox-strength (Aleph) of the waters (Mem) sealed by the covenant mark (Tav) — the mighty deep signed and established, truth as what stands firm from first to last.
חֹק: The fence (Chet) following the sun on the horizon (Qoph) — the wall that tracks the circuit of the sky, the fixed boundary that never moves, the statute.
עֶצֶם: The eye (Ayin) caught by the fish hook (Tsade) in the waters (Mem) — the seeing drawn deep into the mighty current, the hard core that remains, bone and essence.
כֹּחַ: The open palm (Kaph) inside the fence (Chet) — the hand clenched within the inner wall, the contained force waiting to strike, vigor and power.
שָׁלַךְ: The teeth (Shin) goad (Lamed) the open palm (Kaph) — the consuming fire that drives the hand to release, the violent throw that casts away.
נָגַשׁ: The living fish (Nun) carried by the camel (Gimel) into the fire (Shin) — life lifted and brought near to the consuming presence, to approach and draw close.
חֵמָה: The fence (Chet) of the waters (Mem) revealed by breath (He) — the walled-up flood boiling and breathing out through the window, the heat of rage and fury.
חָשַׁב: The fence (Chet) consumed by fire (Shin) inside the house (Beth) — the inner room where thoughts burn and weave together within the dwelling, to think and reckon.
חֵצִי: The fence (Chet) hooks the desire (Tsade) worked by the hand (Yod) — the wall that catches and divides the longing in the working hand, the half, the middle.
קָהָל: The sun on the horizon (Qoph) revealed by breath (He) and driven by the goad (Lamed) — the people who follow the light, breathe together, and are directed as one, the assembly.
אָז: The ox-strength (Aleph) cut by the weapon (Zayin) — the primal moment sliced and fixed by the blade, the decisive then, at that time.
לָכַד: The goad (Lamed) directs the open palm (Kaph) through the door (Dalet) — the driving rod that bends the hand to seize at the threshold, to catch and capture.
בָּטַח: The house (Beth) where the snake (Tet) coils inside the fence (Chet) — the dwelling where what surrounds is contained within the wall, the security of trust.
אֲנַחְנוּ: The ox-strength (Aleph) of the living fish (Nun) inside the fence (Chet), the fish (Nun) connected (Vav) — the strong lives enclosed together and hooked to one another, we.
צֶדֶק: The fish hook of desire (Tsade) at the door (Dalet) following the sun (Qoph) — the righteous longing that enters the pathway and follows the light to the horizon, justice.
יָשָׁר: The hand (Yod) consumed by fire (Shin) at the head (Resh) — the deed refined by flame and set at the highest point, the straight and upright path.
רֶכֶב: The head (Resh) yields the open palm (Kaph) inside the house (Beth) — the chief bending his hand to guide what is within the enclosure, the chariot as a moving house under the rider's control.
נָהָר: The living fish (Nun) revealed by breath (He) at the head (Resh) — the swimming life that breathes and rises to the surface, the great river flowing.
קָדוֹשׁ: The sun on the horizon (Qoph) enters the door (Dalet) connected (Vav) to the consuming fire (Shin) — the one who follows the light through the threshold into the holy flame, sacred and set apart.
בְּכוֹר: The house (Beth) yields the open palm (Kaph) connected (Vav) to the head (Resh) — the family opening its hand and linking to the chief, the firstborn who inherits the headship.
פַּעַם: The mouth (Pe) of the eye (Ayin) in the waters (Mem) — the speaking sight striking the surface of the deep, the footfall that beats like a pulse, a stroke, a time.
לָשׁוֹן: The goad (Lamed) drives the teeth (Shin) connected (Vav) to the living fish (Nun) — the directed flame hooked to swimming life, the tongue as the sharp, living blade in the mouth.
שָׂרַף: The teeth (Shin) of the chief (Resh) speaking through the mouth (Pe) — the consuming fire of the head pouring forth from the edge, to burn utterly.
מַמְלָכָה: The waters (Mem) upon waters (Mem) goaded (Lamed) by the open palm (Kaph) revealed by breath (He) — the doubled might directed by the yielding hand and made visible, the dominion of a kingdom.
תּוֹעֵבַה: The covenant mark (Tav) connected (Vav) to the eye (Ayin) inside the house (Beth) revealed by breath (He) — the sacred sign hooked to a seeing that dwells within and is exposed, the abomination laid bare.
בָּכָה: The house (Beth) yields the open palm (Kaph) and breathes out (He) — the dwelling opening its hands to the window and releasing breath, the weeping that pours from within.
שָׁפַךְ: The teeth (Shin) at the mouth (Pe) yield the open palm (Kaph) — the consuming edge speaking and releasing the hand, the violent spilling forth of what was held.
כָּבַד: The open palm (Kaph) inside the house (Beth) at the door (Dalet) — the yielding hand weighted down in the dwelling at the threshold, too heavy to pass through, honor and glory.
נָבָא: The living fish (Nun) inside the house (Beth) of ox-strength (Aleph) — the swimming life dwelling within the mighty one, the prophet who speaks what surges up from God's house.
מִגְרָשׁ: The waters (Mem) carry (Gimel) the chief (Resh) consumed by fire (Shin) — the mighty force that lifts and drives the head outward through flame, the open land cast out from the city, the suburb.
אָחוֹת: The ox-strength (Aleph) inside the fence (Chet) connected (Vav) and sealed by the covenant (Tav) — the strong one enclosed in the family wall, hooked and marked together, the sister.
קָטַר: The sun on the horizon (Qoph) coiled by the snake (Tet) at the head (Resh) — the circling light wound about the summit, the smoke that spirals upward from the altar.
בּוּשׁ: The house (Beth) connected (Vav) to the consuming fire (Shin) — the dwelling hooked to the devouring flame, the household exposed and burned pale with shame.
גָּדַל: The camel (Gimel) enters the door (Dalet) driven by the goad (Lamed) — the great beast pushing through the gateway and directed onward, to grow large and become great.
יָדָה: The hand (Yod) at the door (Dalet) revealed by breath (He) — the open hand thrown through the threshold into the light, the casting forth of praise and thanksgiving.
שֶׁקֶר: The teeth (Shin) follow the sun (Qoph) to the head (Resh) — the consuming fire chasing a false horizon to a counterfeit chief, the lie that devours by deception.
יָטַב: The hand (Yod) coils the snake (Tet) inside the house (Beth) — the working deed that contains and surrounds what is within the dwelling, making it good and well.
בִּלְתִּי: The house (Beth) goaded (Lamed) toward the covenant (Tav) worked by the hand (Yod) — the dwelling driven toward the mark but stopped by the working hand, the failure that falls short, without.
שַׁבָּת: The teeth (Shin) inside the house (Beth) sealed by the covenant (Tav) — the consuming fire that rests within the dwelling under the mark of the sacred sign, the sabbath rest.
נֶגֶב: The living fish (Nun) carried by the camel (Gimel) into the house (Beth) — the life borne by the desert beast into the dry dwelling, the parched south country.
עַמּוּד: The eye (Ayin) of the waters (Mem) connected (Vav) to the door (Dalet) — the mighty seeing hooked and standing at the threshold, the pillar that rises and holds the gateway open.
אֲבִישָׁלוֹם: The ox-strength (Aleph) of the house (Beth) works (Yod) through consuming fire (Shin) goaded (Lamed) and connected (Vav) to the waters (Mem) — the father of peace whose mighty household labors through flame toward the deep, Absalom.
לָבַשׁ: The goad (Lamed) drives into the house (Beth) consumed by fire (Shin) — the directed rod pressing fabric into the dwelling and covering it with flame-tested cloth, to clothe and wrap around.
עָפָר: The eye (Ayin) at the mouth (Pe) of the head (Resh) — the seeing at the speaking edge of the chief, what crumbles from the surface and scatters, dust.
כָּנָף: The open palm (Kaph) of the living fish (Nun) at the mouth (Pe) — the yielding hand of the darting life at the edge, the wing that bends and stretches to the outermost border.
שֹׁמְרוֹן: The fire/teeth (Shin) consume the waters (Mem) at the head (Resh), hooked (Vav) to living activity (Nun) — the watchfire burning over the mighty hilltop, a city perched and guarded, Samaria on its peak.
רַק: The head (Resh) follows the sun on the horizon (Qoph) — the chief stripped to a thin line at the edge, only a silhouette remaining, nothing but the barest outline.
שְׁמֹנֶה: The fire/teeth (Shin) consume the waters (Mem), the living fish (Nun) revealed through the window (He) — seven consumed and one more life appears at the opening, the eighth emerging from the deep.
אָמַן: The ox-strength (Aleph) stands in the waters (Mem) with the living fish (Nun) — the strong one planted firm amid the chaos of swimming life, steadfast and faithful, built up to endure.
נָחַם: The living fish (Nun) enclosed by the fence (Chet) sighs into the waters (Mem) — the active life walled in and breathing heavy over the deep, the groan of comfort and repentance.
