יִצְחָק: The hand (Yod) catches with a fish hook (Tsade) inside the fence (Chet), following the sun (Qoph) — the working hand that hooks joy behind the wall at the horizon, Isaac the child of laughter pulled from impossibility.
סָפַר: The prop/support (Samech) steadies the mouth (Pe) of the chief (Resh) — the pillar that holds up the speaking head, the act of counting and recounting aloud what the leader declares.
כֶּבֶשׂ: The open palm (Kaph) yields inside the house (Beth) to the consuming teeth (Shin) — the tame creature bending in the dwelling, given over to be consumed, the lamb led gently to fire.
שְׁלִישִׁי: The teeth (Shin) goad (Lamed) the hand (Yod), then the teeth (Shin) work the hand (Yod) again — consumed, directed, worked, consumed again — the third as the doubled process.
יָתַר: The hand (Yod) marks a covenant (Tav) at the head (Resh) — the working deed sealed at the top, the remainder that juts over the edge, what is left over after the covenant is set.
עַמּוֹן: The eye (Ayin) sees the mighty waters (Mem) hooked (Vav) to the living fish (Nun) — the watching eye over the turbulent tribal waters, Ammon as the people who see and teem at the edge.
חֻקָּה: The fence (Chet) follows the sun on the horizon (Qoph), revealed through the window (He) — the boundary that tracks the sun's circuit and is seen at every opening, the statute carved into the wall.
יָרׇבְעָם: The hand (Yod) of the chief (Resh) inside the house (Beth) sees (Ayin) the mighty waters (Mem) — the working leader whose household eyes the multitude, Jeroboam who contended with the people.
תָּמִיד: The covenant mark (Tav) over the waters (Mem), worked by the hand (Yod) through the door (Dalet) — the sign planted in the deep, hand pushing through the threshold without ceasing, a perpetual motion.
מַרְאֶה: The waters (Mem) of the chief (Resh) ox-strength (Aleph) revealed through the window (He) — the mighty head's power made visible through the opening, a vision beheld at the window.
גָּאַל: The camel (Gimel) carries the ox-strength (Aleph) driven by the goad (Lamed) — the one who lifts and bears the primal burden, the kinsman-redeemer who shoulders the cost and brings the lost one home.
שָׁלַם: The consuming fire (Shin) directed by the goad (Lamed) into the waters (Mem) — the teeth of destruction guided toward the deep until the burning ceases, peace as the fire made whole and still.
רוּץ: The head (Resh) hooked (Vav) to the fish hook's desire (Tsade) — the chief connected to urgent longing, the body pulled forward by the hook of pursuit, running headlong.
בְּעַד: The house (Beth) seen by the eye (Ayin) through the door (Dalet) — the dwelling perceived through the threshold, what is within and behind and through, the passage from inside to out.
כֹּל: The open palm (Kaph) of the goad (Lamed) — the yielded hand directed outward toward everything, the encompassing reach that gathers all.
שָׁכַח: The consuming teeth (Shin) open the palm (Kaph) toward the fence (Chet) — the fire that releases the hand's grip behind the wall, what was held slipping into the hidden room, forgotten.
בָּמָה: The house (Beth) rises over the waters (Mem), revealed through the window (He) — the dwelling set above the mighty chaos, beheld from afar, the high place where the wave crests.
כָּפַר: The open palm (Kaph) covers the mouth (Pe) of the chief (Resh) — the yielding hand pressed over the speaking head, silencing the accusation, the covering that atones and hides the guilt.
מְעַט: The waters (Mem) seen by the eye (Ayin) coiled by the snake (Tet) — the mighty flood reduced to what the eye can spot in a serpent's coil, a small and contained thing.
רֹחַב: The head (Resh) beyond the fence (Chet) inside the house (Beth) — the chief looking past the wall into the wide dwelling, the breadth of space that opens when barriers fall.
רָעָב: The head (Resh) sees with the eye (Ayin) an empty house (Beth) — the chief who looks and finds the dwelling bare, hunger as the sight of nothing inside the home.
קִרְאָה: The sun on the horizon (Qoph) draws the chief (Resh) and ox-strength (Aleph) toward the window (He) — the horizon-call pulling the strong leader to the opening, the encounter that comes to meet you face to face.
אֱדֹם: The ox-strength (Aleph) through the door (Dalet) into the waters (Mem) — the strong one entering the threshold of the red chaotic deep, Edom as the ruddy earth-power striding into the flood.
יַעַן: The hand (Yod) of the eye (Ayin) upon the living fish (Nun) — the working sight fixed on moving life, heeding because the hand sees the reason swimming beneath.
עוֹר: The eye (Ayin) hooked (Vav) to the head (Resh) — the visible surface connected to the body's chief, the skin as the outer covering that the eye sees first.
יַחַד: The hand (Yod) inside the fence (Chet) at the door (Dalet) — the working deed enclosed within the same wall and entering the same threshold, together as one unit behind a single gate.
שְׁבִיעִי: The teeth (Shin) inside the house (Beth) worked by the hand (Yod), seen by the eye (Ayin), and worked by the hand (Yod) again — the consuming rest inside the dwelling, the seventh day when the eye sees the completed work.
שָׂבַע: The teeth (Shin) consuming inside the house (Beth) until the eye (Ayin) is satisfied — the fire of eating within the dwelling until the seeing is full, satiated at last.
מֵהֶם: The waters (Mem) revealed through the window (He) back to the waters (Mem) — from the deep, beheld, and returning to the deep, the origin flowing out and pointing back to its source.
שָׁרַת: The fire/teeth (Shin) of the chief (Resh) sealed by the covenant mark (Tav) — the consuming devotion of the head, signed and bound to serve, the minister who attends with burning loyalty.
עֵשָׂו: The eye (Ayin) consumed by fire (Shin) and hooked (Vav) — the one who sees and burns and is snared, Esau caught by appetite and tethered to the moment.
חֲמוֹר: The fence (Chet) holds the mighty waters (Mem) hooked (Vav) to the head (Resh) — the walled beast carrying the weight of chaos, connected to its master's head, the donkey bearing burdens behind the barrier.
אֹרֶךְ: The ox-strength (Aleph) at the head (Resh), bent by the open palm (Kaph) — the primal power stretching from the first to the yielding end, length as the distance the strong arm reaches.
מָלַט: The waters (Mem) goaded (Lamed) past the coiling snake (Tet) — the mighty flow driven around the serpent's grip, slipping smooth and free, the escape that slides away from danger.
יֶתֶר: The hand (Yod) of the covenant mark (Tav) at the head (Resh) — the working deed sealed at the top that hangs over the edge, the remnant and excess that overhangs the rest.
טָהֵר: The coiled snake (Tet) breathes through the window (He) toward the head (Resh) — the contained impurity exhaled through the opening and released to the chief, purified by the breath that passes through.
אֱלָהּ: The ox-strength (Aleph) goaded (Lamed) and revealed by breath (He) — the mighty one driven forward and made known through the window, God as the strong power that teaches and is beheld.
הָפַךְ: Behold (He) the mouth (Pe) bending the open palm (Kaph) — the revealed word that turns the yielding hand over, the overturning that flips everything the mouth has spoken.
רָעַע: The head (Resh) of the eye (Ayin) seeing the eye (Ayin) — the chief perception doubled back on itself, evil as sight reflecting sight, the shattering that comes from seeing only ruin.
שִׂמְחָה: The fire/teeth (Shin) over the waters (Mem) enclosed by the fence (Chet), revealed through the window (He) — the burning delight that rises from the deep, contained and then shown forth, joy bursting through the opening.
דַּעַת: The door (Dalet) of the eye (Ayin) sealed by the covenant mark (Tav) — the threshold of perception marked and established, knowledge as the signed doorway through which seeing passes.
טָהוֹר: The coiled snake (Tet) breathes (He), hooked (Vav) to the head (Resh) — what was wrapped in impurity exhales through the window and connects to the chief, pure as the venom drawn out and the head made clean.
פֶּשַׁע: The mouth (Pe) consumed by fire (Shin) before the eye (Ayin) — the speaking edge devoured by its own flame in full view, revolt as the word that burns and is seen by all.
אַחְאָב: The ox-strength (Aleph) fenced (Chet) from the ox-strength (Aleph) inside the house (Beth) — the strong one walled off from the first power within the dwelling, Ahab as the brother of the father divided from strength.
זָנָה: The weapon (Zayin) cuts the living fish (Nun) open to the window (He) — the blade that severs life and exposes it, adultery as the cutting of the living bond laid bare for all to see.
כֶּרֶם: The open palm (Kaph) of the chief (Resh) over the waters (Mem) — the yielding hand of the head tending the mighty flow, the vineyard as the cupped hand that catches the rain for the vines.
גֵּר: The camel (Gimel) before the chief (Resh) — the wandering beast arriving at the head of the household, the stranger who carries his life on his back and stands before the master.
קָצֶה: The sun on the horizon (Qoph) hooks the desire (Tsade), revealed at the window (He) — the farthest point where the horizon's line catches the last longing, the extremity beheld at the edge of sight.
עֹז: The eye (Ayin) of the weapon (Zayin) — the perception behind the blade, the seeing that wields the cutting edge, strength as the force that both perceives and strikes.
חֶלֶב: The inner room (Chet) goads (Lamed) toward the house (Beth) — what is directed from the hidden interior into the dwelling, the richest part stored deep inside, the fat reserved within.
זְרוֹעַ: The weapon (Zayin) at the head (Resh) hooked (Vav) to the eye (Ayin) — the blade of the chief connected to the seeing, the arm as the striking limb that extends power and sight outward.
שִׁבְעִים: The teeth (Shin) inside the house (Beth) seen by the eye (Ayin), worked by the hand (Yod) in the waters (Mem) — the consuming fullness within the dwelling, watched and worked through the mighty deep, seventy as the completed cycle of seven multiplied.
סָגַר: The prop/support (Samech) lifts the camel (Gimel) before the chief (Resh) — the pillar that carries the burden and blocks the head's way, shutting the entrance where the laden beast stands firm.
תָּמִים: The covenant mark (Tav) over the waters (Mem) worked by the hand (Yod) through waters (Mem) again — the sign set in the deep, labored through, and returning to the deep unblemished, entire and without spot.
כְּרוּב: The open palm (Kaph) of the chief (Resh) hooked (Vav) inside the house (Beth) — the yielding hand of the head connected within the dwelling, the cherub as the bowing guardian stationed inside the holy place.
מַלְכוּת: The waters (Mem) goaded (Lamed) by the open palm (Kaph), hooked (Vav) to the covenant mark (Tav) — the mighty flow directed by the bending hand and fastened to the sign, kingdom as the ruled dominion sealed by covenant.
חָרָה: The fence (Chet) of the chief (Resh) revealed at the window (He) — the inner wall of the head burning and showing through, anger as the hidden fire that glows and is seen.
שָׁמַד: The consuming teeth (Shin) of the waters (Mem) crash through the door (Dalet) — the fire-flood that devours the threshold, desolation as the force that destroys every entrance.
כְּנַעַן: The open palm (Kaph) of the living fish (Nun) seen by the eye (Ayin) and the fish (Nun) again — the yielding hand of the swarming life perceived and teeming, Canaan as the land of traders whose busy hands never rest.
עֵבֶר: The eye (Ayin) inside the house (Beth) of the chief (Resh) — the sight passing through the dwelling of the head, the other side as what the eye sees when it crosses the household threshold.
חָלָל: The fence (Chet) pierced by the goad (Lamed) and the goad (Lamed) again — the wall driven through twice, the double puncture that breaches the inner room, the slain one pierced and profaned.
יֶלֶד: The hand (Yod) goaded (Lamed) through the door (Dalet) — the deed directed toward the threshold and pushed through, the child as what is brought forth through the opening into the world.
עֵצָה: The eye (Ayin) hooks the desire (Tsade), revealed through the window (He) — the perception that catches what is sought and shows it at the opening, counsel as the seeing that draws wisdom into view.
שֶׁקֶל: The consuming teeth (Shin) follow the sun (Qoph) to the goad (Lamed) — the fire that tracks the horizon and is directed to the measure, the shekel as the weight that the consuming market drives toward the standard.
עָנָן: The eye (Ayin) of the living fish (Nun) and the fish (Nun) again — the seeing of life upon life, the cloud as the teeming moisture that the eye perceives swimming above, layer upon layer.
אֱמֹרִי: The ox-strength (Aleph) in the waters (Mem) at the head (Resh) worked by the hand (Yod) — the mighty one rising from the deep to the summit, the Amorite as the highland dweller whose strength commands the peaks.
שֶׁלֶם: The consuming fire (Shin) goaded (Lamed) into the waters (Mem) — the teeth directed to the deep until the burning is quenched, the peace offering as the sacrifice that makes all whole.
שִׁיר: The teeth (Shin) worked by the hand (Yod) at the head (Resh) — the consuming fire shaped by the working hand into the chief melody, the song as fire made into music by skillful hands.
לָמַד: The goad (Lamed) drives the waters (Mem) through the door (Dalet) — the ox goad that pushes the mighty flow through the threshold, teaching as the directed force that moves knowledge through the opening.
דֶּלֶת: The door (Dalet) goaded (Lamed) to the covenant mark (Tav) — the threshold directed toward the sign, the swinging door that moves on its hinge toward the mark and back again.
פֵּאָה: The mouth (Pe) of the ox-strength (Aleph) breathing (He) — the speaking edge of the first power revealed, the corner as the boundary where the strong voice echoes from the side.
שָׁמֵם: The consuming teeth (Shin) of the waters (Mem) and the waters (Mem) again — fire crashing through wave after wave, the double desolation of flood and flame, the stunned emptiness left behind.
בָּעַר: The house (Beth) where the eye (Ayin) sees the chief (Resh) — the dwelling where perception meets the head and ignites, the kindling that blazes when the seeing eye finds fuel within the home.
רָחוֹק: The head (Resh) behind the fence (Chet), hooked (Vav) to the sun on the horizon (Qoph) — the chief separated by the wall and connected only to the farthest horizon, remote as the distance between the walled self and the setting sun.
קָנָה: The sun on the horizon (Qoph) draws the living fish (Nun) to the window (He) — the horizon pulling life toward the opening, to acquire as the act of reaching for what rises and making it one's own.
פָּלַל: The mouth (Pe) goaded (Lamed) and goaded (Lamed) again — the speaking directed and redirected, prayer as the doubled turning of the word toward its target, judging and interceding in the same breath.
יְהוֹשָׁפָט: The hand (Yod) reveals (He) and hooks (Vav) the consuming fire (Shin) to the mouth (Pe) surrounded by the snake (Tet) — the working hand of the revealed one connecting the fire to the spoken judgment, Jehoshaphat whose God judges.
שִׁית: The consuming fire (Shin) worked by the hand (Yod) sealed by the covenant mark (Tav) — the teeth set by the working hand upon the mark, to place as the deliberate act of setting something where it is signed to be.
בַּעַל: The house (Beth) where the eye (Ayin) wields the goad (Lamed) — the dwelling in which the seeing one directs and drives, the master who owns and commands from within his household.
פֹּה: The mouth (Pe) breathing (He) — the speaking that exhales and reveals, here as the place where the word is breathed, this very spot where the mouth opens.
קָלַל: The sun on the horizon (Qoph) goads (Lamed) and goads again (Lamed) — the horizon driving twice with its sharp point, the thing made light and swift by double goading, the curse that cuts both ways.
הָמוֹן: Behold (He) the waters (Mem) hooked (Vav) to the living fish (Nun) — the revealed deep connected to swarming life, the roaring noise of the multitude churning like a sea full of fish.
לָא: The goad (Lamed) turned back from the ox (Aleph) — the directing stick that finds no strength to drive, the negation where the goad meets nothing, not, no, without.
סָתַר: The prop/support (Samech) sealed by the covenant mark (Tav) at the head (Resh) — the pillar covered with the sign before the chief, hiding as the act of surrounding the marked thing and concealing it from the leader's sight.
עָזַר: The eye (Ayin) of the weapon (Zayin) at the chief (Resh) — the seeing blade that surrounds the head and guards it, help as the watchful sword-arm that circles the leader.
מָשַׁל: The waters (Mem) consumed by fire (Shin) and directed by the goad (Lamed) — the mighty deep devoured and driven, to rule as the power that both consumes the chaos and directs its course.
גּוּר: The camel (Gimel) hooked (Vav) to the chief (Resh) — the wandering beast tethered to the head of the household, the sojourner who dwells in awe at the tent peg of the master.
עוּר: The eye (Ayin) hooked (Vav) to the head (Resh) — the seeing connected to the chief who was sleeping, the eye pulling upward to wake the head, to rouse and stir from slumber.
הֵיכָל: Behold (He) the hand (Yod) of the open palm (Kaph) directed by the goad (Lamed) — the revealed work of the yielding hand driven toward its purpose, the palace as the great hall where every hand bows and every goad points to the throne.
